title: Conjecture 5.3 of [24] (Induced Arithmetic Removal Lemma)
statement_text: An induced arithmetic removal lemma holds in general: for a fixed finite collection of $r$-colored arithmetic patterns, every coloring with $o(1)$ density of each such pattern can be recolored on an $o(1)$-fraction of the ambient space so that no such pattern remains.
context_text: The paper identifies this as a natural common extension of both the induced graph removal lemma and the arithmetic removal lemma. It is noted as not currently known, with [24, Conjecture 5.3] cited as one explicit formulation. The technique of deducing arithmetic removal from graph removal does not appear to extend to the induced setting.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for complexity 1 patterns over finite fields $\mathbb{F}_q$: for every $\epsilon > 0$ there exists $\delta > 0$ such that if $\phi : V \setminus \{0\} \to [r]$ has $H$-density at most $\delta$ for every $H \in \mathcal{H}$, then there is a recoloring $\phi'$ that is $\mathcal{H}$-free and differs from $\phi$ on at most $\epsilon|V|$ elements (Theorem 1.14).
notes: The exact statement of [24, Conjecture 5.3] is not reproduced verbatim in this paper; statement_text is reconstructed from the surrounding prose. Bibliography entry for [24] is not present in the extracted text, so attributed_to cannot be made more precise.
paper_title: Induced arithmetic removal: complexity 1 patterns over finite fields

title: Open Problem (Extending Induced Arithmetic Removal to Higher Complexity and General Groups)
statement_text: Extend the induced arithmetic removal lemma beyond complexity 1 patterns over finite fields to higher complexity patterns and to general abelian groups (even the case of $\mathbb{Z}$ is open).
context_text: After establishing Theorem 1.14 for complexity 1 patterns over $\mathbb{F}_q$, the authors note that extending to higher complexity and to general groups remains open. They observe that for affine-invariant patterns (which avoid a regularisation difficulty near the origin), the problem for bounded complexity patterns over finite fields has been solved, but the general induced setting is still unresolved.
paper_title: Induced arithmetic removal: complexity 1 patterns over finite fields

title: Informal Conjecture (Induced Arithmetic Removal for Arbitrary Abelian Groups)
statement_text: A statement analogous to Theorem 1.14 holds for arbitrary patterns with coefficients in $\mathbb{Z}$ over arbitrary abelian groups, subject to appropriate caveats concerning infinite complexity patterns and trivial solutions.
context_text: Immediately following the statement of the main theorem (Theorem 1.14) and its remark on tower-type bounds, the authors write: 'We conjecture that a similar statement holds for arbitrary patterns (with coefficients in $\mathbb{Z}$) in arbitrary abelian groups.' They decline to give a precise formulation due to unresolved issues with infinite complexity and trivial solutions.
notes: Authors explicitly decline to state a precise conjecture: 'we do not state a precise conjecture here'. Classified as Informal accordingly.
paper_title: Induced arithmetic removal: complexity 1 patterns over finite fields

title: Informal Conjecture (Polynomial Quantitative Bound for Complexity 1 Systems)
statement_text: For complexity 1 systems, the dependence of $\delta$ on $\epsilon$ in Definition 1.10 satisfies $\delta = \epsilon^{O_A(1)}$, i.e., polynomial dependence.
context_text: In Section 1.2, after defining complexity 1 (Definition 1.10), the authors remark that the general dependence of $\delta$ on $\epsilon$ is a wide open problem and state conjecturally that polynomial dependence holds. Reference [20] is cited for further discussion of this quantitative question.
notes: Stated as 'conjecturally' in running prose without a labelled environment and without explicit attribution to named individuals; reference [20] is cited for discussion but no author names are given in the extracted text.
paper_title: Induced arithmetic removal: complexity 1 patterns over finite fields
